hints.surgit: 'Surgit' means 'she rises' but does not appear in this sentence — the form here is 'surge'
hints.surrexit: 'Surrexit' means 'she arose' but does not appear in this sentence

hints.to run: That would be 'currere' — 'surge' comes from 'surgere' (to rise)
hints.to go out: That would be 'exire' — 'surge' comes from 'surgere' (to rise)
hints.to heal: That would be 'sanare' — 'surge' comes from 'surgere' (to rise)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'surgit' (3rd pers.) — 'surge' is a command
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'surgat' — 'surge' is imperative singular
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'surrexit' — 'surge' is present imperative

hints.rego, regere, rexi, rectum: These are the parts of 'regere' (to rule/guide)
hints.curro, currere, cucurri, cursum: These are the parts of 'currere' (to run)
hints.erigo, erigere, erexi, erectum: These are the parts of 'erigere' (to raise up) — related but different from 'surgere'

hints.nominative: While 'puella' is identical in nominative, Jesus is addressing the girl directly — vocative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'puella' in form, but context shows direct address — vocative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'puellam' — the girl is being addressed, so vocative

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'puellae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'puellae' ends in -ae
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'puellae' ends in -ae

steps.prompt: The imperative 'surge' is addressed to how many people?

hints.two or more (plural): Plural imperative would be 'surgite' — 'surge' is singular
hints.it is not imperative: 'Surge' is indeed imperative — a direct command to the girl
hints.unspecified: The imperative form specifies number: 'surge' is specifically singular

hints.1st conjugation: 1st conjugation has infinitive in -are — 'surgere' ends in -ere (short e)
hints.2nd conjugation: 2nd conjugation has infinitive in -ēre (long e) — 'surgere' has short -ere
hints.4th conjugation: 4th conjugation has infinitive in -ire — 'surgere' ends in -ere
